# SRC018 - Fogg Behavior Model

## Metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Section | Buyer Psychology |
| Authors / source | BJ Fogg / Stanford Behavior Design Lab |
| Year | 2009/current |
| Source type | Behavior design theory |
| Quality | High |
| URL | [https://behaviordesign.stanford.edu/resources/fogg-behavior-model](https://behaviordesign.stanford.edu/resources/fogg-behavior-model) |

## Bias / limitation

Persuasive-design lens; simplify with care.

## Core takeaway

Behavior happens when motivation, ability, and prompt converge.

## How the marketing agent should use it

Use to diagnose why clicks/trials/conversions fail: motivation, ability, or prompt.

## Tactics unlocked

- Convert source insight into a campaign hypothesis.
- Translate the hypothesis into one ad angle, one landing-page proof section, and one measurement plan.
- Record whether the tactic worked in `memory/tactic_performance.md`.

## Agent instruction

When this source is relevant, write one sentence explaining **why this source applies to the buyer state** before using it.
